item.title: Restarting Nuclear Power Plants: Preserving and Restoring Value - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:Recent growth in electricity demand, expanding carbon-free energy goals, and renewed concerns about energy security are driving utilities to reconsider nuclear power plants that have been permanently shut down or are approaching retirement. EPRI examines the technical, regulatory, and business factors that influence whether these facilities can return to service and what steps [âŠ]

item.title: Why Grid Models Need a Solid Data Foundation - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:By Chris Warren The distribution grid isnât what it used to be. Traditionally, the lower-voltage grid carried electricity in one direction from large power plants to businesses and residents who needed it. Though distribution grids have always been inherently complex, the one-way flow of electrons from power plants and the transmission grid to [âŠ]

item.title: EPRI's Roadmap for a Rapidly Growing Battery Storage Industry - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:By Chris Warren Installations of battery energy storage are rapidly accelerating across the globe. According to the International Energy Agency (IEA), 108 GW of new battery storage was deployed globally in 2025, an increase of 40% over 2024 and 11 times more than in 2021. A similar story is playing out in the [âŠ]

item.title: The Long Wait - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:How EPRI helped Japanâs nuclear industry navigate an extended shutdown, modernize for the future, and generate valuable lessons for the rest of the world The second in a series of three articles about EPRIâs long-term commitment to the Japanese nuclear industry post-Fukushima examines the days and weeks after the 2011 tsunami. By Chris [âŠ]

item.title: The Electric Airport - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:A new EPRI offering draws on decades of experience and wide-ranging expertise to help airports prepare for an electric liftoff. By Chris Warren Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is a city within a city. The airport covers 5.5 square miles and regularly serves well over 70 million passengers annually, making it one of [âŠ]

item.title: Fire Safety Considerations for Electric Vehicles and Charging Stations - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:What Fleet Managers Need to Know As electric vehicles (EVs) become an increasingly common part of commercial and utility fleets, questions around fire safety arise. While available data indicate that fires involving electric vehicles may occur less frequently than those involving internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles, EVs present unique hazardsâparticularly when a fire [âŠ]

item.title: Beyond Billing: How Utilities Can Unlock the Full Value of Advanced Metering Infrastructure - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:By Chris Warren Judging by the numbers alone, the rollout of advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) has been an undeniable success. Utilities such as Pacific Gas & Electric (PG&E) and Florida Power & Light (FP&L) were among the first to launch ambitious smart meter deployments in the mid- to late 2000s. It was the [âŠ]

item.title: Synthetic Customer Load Data: Approaches, Limitations, and the Role of Generative AI - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:Electric utilities are navigating a period of rapid change driven by electrification, renewable energy integration, climate variability, and evolving customer behavior. These trends are placing new demands on planning processes that have historically relied on empirical load data, such as advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) records or curated reference datasets. While these data sources [âŠ]

item.title: Why Data Readiness is One of Utilitiesâ Most Urgent AI Challenges - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:Realizing AI potential starts with getting data right. By Chris Warren In a relatively short period of time, artificial intelligence (AI) has become the centerpiece of discussions across most industries, including the utility industry. Much of the recent focus has been on utilitiesâ ability to meet the growing demand for electricity coming from [âŠ]

item.title: Epri Journal No 2 Spring
item.description: ïżœïżœïżœ;ïżœïżœïżœïżœ9^{ïżœïżœ=?ïżœdïżœïżœïżœïżœcCsïżœïżœ$tNáĄ»_?iïżœfïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœUïżœïżœïżœKïżœë§yïżœ%ÏG8ïżœÌžïżœ'y.$ïżœ$ïżœ{OïżœOÔeNïżœCïżœ61RuïżœïżœïżœMïżœwïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœ)ïżœćż-ïżœZNïżœïżœïżœïżœRïżœ'ïżœCu]\ïżœïżœ(dïżœïżœïżœMïżœĘč9;ïżœïżœïżœ8fdïżœïżœZïżœĆ·ïżœïżœjïżœWm!\:ïżœïżœOMbM ïżœïżœvïżœïżœIïżœkwȚïżœïżœ\ïżœ4ïżœ=ïżœYïżœ0"ïżœïżœçșȘsÛïżœÍŸïżœïżœ%ïżœïżœïżœZÒšq%ïżœïżœmÇčïżœïżœ!MïżœïżœïżœÇŹÊ¶ïżœïżœïżœïżœp-ïżœïżœ9.UïżœŰźïżœâąïżœïżœïżœA-ïżœïżœ{x8}ïżœJïżœïżœoïżœïżœ8ïżœïżœ=0ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœ6?u7ïżœ{ïżœÍ«ïżœïżœbX\&EH1ïżœ\:ïżœdc,ïżœïżœi6 ïżœïżœ-ïżœ+nïżœïżœ| ïżœOUïżœ1ïżœïżœaïżœx_-oïżœ~ïżœïżœïżœeïżœïżœïżœŚ/ïżœEïżœ8ïżœïżœïżœ1ïżœvPNïżœïżœŐ%Y`ïżœg rïżœ' ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœE#ïżœ*xïżœ5ïżœc<ïżœ'ïżœÇšïżœo$ïżœïżœ.ïżœïżœGïżœïżœ;ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœX~>eïżœïżœïżœÜïżœŃ”,YïżœIïżœKkhïżœKïżœ.hïżœKElïżœïżœïżœ\"ïżœïżœa!fïżœ.ïżœïżœnpïżœïżœHïżœ*$ïżœïżœïżœIïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœ7ïżœïżœbïżœ&qjGPïżœïżœïżœIïżœZ-*|ïżœLïżœRïżœ:ïżœGCRïżœjQuL 7IEFSd' 5ïżœïżœïżœïżœZ9ïżœgïżœïżœF_ïżœ{>ïżœÓqkïżœïżœïżœmïżœïżœïżœŰTN#ïżœX.ïżœhïżœïżœZ%eX9ïżœ!ïżœßïżœâą§SïżœF~\ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœÉźïżœïżœpïżœPïżœr-ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœ"ïżœ?QD5 ïżœïżœMïżœ Aïżœ,`ïżœ/ïżœïżœ-!ïżœïżœïżœFïżœÙĄVMïżœbSOïżœÜïżœ2ïżœïżœ%/ïżœqïżœïżœïżœI7'ÊźïżœïżœK%+ïżœïżœ|ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœDrxvïżœNïżœïżœ>_dSÍŻïżœ-Uïżœïżœmïżœ,ïżœïżœïżœïżœ\ïżœNïżœZfkïżœpoïżœïżœ<ïżœïżœïżœ7)ïżœïżœZ)5$ïżœïżœ<ïżœïżœ&UïżœĐ±ïżœ%ïżœïżœïżœïżœ 8ïżœ:ïżœïżœȚ±ïżœïżœïżœÓïżœmïżœo{qïżœïżœs d%ïżœïżœïżœïżœ[ïżœ;ïżœïżœ#ïżœïżœHïżœïżœ|>Eïżœïżœïżœ1ïżœïżœ5ïżœïżœ" ïżœïżœ8Xïżœïżœïżœïżœ~ïżœd:kïżœïżœïżœĘźïżœïżœ&ïżœïżœ\ïżœ[a7ïżœ+ïżœïżœÛ±$ïżœwïżœïżœ>Zïżœm,ïżœïżœïżœ4ïżœY>ïżœ5ïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœïżœUïżœïżœ7ïżœïżœEïżœ>ïżœïżœïżœXïżœ

item.title: PJM Board Approves Transmission Project Addition, Changes To Support Grid Reliability | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: The PJM Board of Managers has authorized an immediate-need reliability project and changes to existing projects in the Regional Transmission Expansion Plan (RTEP) to help provide the most efficient, economical and reliable supply of power for the 67 million people PJM serves across 13 states and the District of Columbia.

item.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update â July 15, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: Maximum Generation Alert Issued for July 16; Hot Weather Alert Continues Through July 17

item.title: PJM Capacity Auction Procures 138,318 MW of Generation Resources as Work Continues To Address Growing Electricity Demand | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: Price Comes in at FERC-Approved Cap, Down 2.5%; Total Capacity Cleared Is 6,831 MW Short of Reliability Requirement

item.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update â July 14, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: Maximum Generation Alert Issued for July 15; Hot Weather Alert Extended Through July 17

item.title: Hot Weather Alert Issued for July 14â17 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: PJM has issued a Hot Weather Alert for the entire region PJM serves for July 14â17 ahead of expected 90-plus-degree weather.

item.title: PJM Serves Load Through Record-Breaking July Heat | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: PJM maintained reliable operations July 2 through a preliminary energy use record, requiring all available generation to keep the power flowing.

item.title: Day-Long Planning Process Education Session Set for July 16 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: PJM is hosting a special session of the Transmission Expansion Advisory Committee on July 16 to provide an overview of the annual planning process for a general audience, including how PJM determines when new transmission is needed, how projects are selected and how the public can participate.

item.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update: July 3 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: Hot Weather Alert Remains in Effect Through July 4 and into July 5 for Mid-Atlantic and Dominion Zones

item.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update â July 2, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: Maximum Generation Alert Issued for July 3; Hot Weather Alert Remains in Effect Through July 4

item.title: PJM Hot Weather Operations Update â July 1, 2026 | PJM Inside Lines
item.description: Hot Weather Alert Continues Through at Least July 3; Maximum Generation and Load Management Alert Issued for July 2
